{
  "communicative_function": "enumerating and characterizing a second adverse mental or spiritual state",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "human souls can be in distinct aversive or hostile conditions",
    "aversion or contrary desires can tend toward another person's harm",
    "anger exemplifies such a soul-state",
    "this state is categorically different from a prior unnamed first condition"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent within an archaic classificatory framework but syntactically elliptical and reliant on unstated prior context",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the surrounding classificatory scheme, intended audience, and definitions of 'soul' and 'prejudice' are known and accepted",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the fragment is presented as self-contained modern psychological description, or its evaluative stance on anger is taken as empirical fact rather than normative claim"
}
